title: Reading Signed Text
subtitle: Recognition, syntax, and spatial reference in signed-language literacy
summary: Second research-facing paper for the Sutton SignWriting Language and Literacy Research series.
synopsis: This entry asks the next hard-science question after The Four Language Pathways: what reading would mean in a signed writing system. It outlines a research-facing account of recognition, lexical comparison, layout direction, syntax, spatial reference, and the limits of current evidence, with the vertical-layout history sourced to Sutton's published SignWriting records.
